title: Punjabi Butter Chicken

subs.name: 400 g paneer, seared 2 minutes per side; reduce final simmer to 5 minutes for a vegetarian version
ghee for a more aromatic, traditional finish
1 tbsp almond paste or skip entirely; the sauce will be thinner
4 tbsp thick coconut milk stirred in at the end; the flavour shifts slightly sweet

steps.text: Mix the chicken with the curd, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, 1 tsp red chilli powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, lemon juice and salt. Turn to coat, then leave to marinate for 20 minutes at room temperature or overnight covered in the fridge.

Heat 1 tsp oil in a tawa or wide frying pan over high flame. Sear the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer without crowding, cooking 3–4 minutes per side until dark spots form on the surface. The chicken need not be fully cooked through at this stage. Set aside.

In the same pan, melt 1 tbsp butter and fry the onion with cardamom, cloves and cinnamon until the onion turns soft and lightly golden. Add the cashews and the remaining ginger-garlic paste and cook 2 minutes. Add the tomatoes and cook, stirring, until they fully break down and oil separates, about 10 minutes.

Cool the mixture slightly, then blend completely smooth in a mixie. Press through a fine sieve and discard the seeds and husks; this extra step is what makes the sauce velvety. Rinse the blender with 50 ml water and add that too.

Melt the remaining butter in a kadai over medium flame. Pour in the strained sauce and cook until it deepens in colour and butter rises at the edges, about 5–6 minutes. Add the remaining red chilli powder and half the remaining garam masala.
Add the seared chicken along with 100 ml hot water. Stir, cover and simmer on medium-low flame for 15 minutes until the chicken is completely cooked and the sauce clings to every piece.
Rub the kasuri methi in your palms and stir it in along with the cream and the remaining garam masala. Simmer 3 more minutes uncovered. Taste for salt and serve with naan, butter roti or jeera rice.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: cream → 4 tbsp thick coconut milk stirred in at the end; the flavour shifts slightly sweet; cashew → 1 tbsp almond paste or skip entirely; the sauce will be thinner; chicken → 400 g paneer, seared 2 minutes per side; reduce final simmer to 5 minutes for a vegetarian version; butter → ghee for a more aromatic, traditional finish
